nodes.label: AI as efficiency tool for routine tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI to speed up tasks such as calculating, data entry, and writing emails and drafts.

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner requiring oversight
nodes.label_negative: AI as autonomous delegate without oversight
nodes.grounding_span: I never delegate tasks for it to handle completely. I always collaborate with AI to ensure that everything it provides is correct and factual.

nodes.label: AI reliability and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: AI often provides incorrect information and fact-checking its response takes just as much, or more time, than finding the answer myself using credible sources.

nodes.label: AI use for non-confidential tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to do any tasks that include confidential information as I worry about how my data is shared.

nodes.label: AI as writing assistant preserving skill
nodes.label_negative: AI as writing replacement degrading skill
nodes.grounding_span: For tasks such as writing emails, what I've been trying to do as of recently is give AI a very quick and rough draft of what I would like to say and let it polish it for me, instead of relying on it to come up with the entire thing.

nodes.label: Productivity and time efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: Any time I can use AI to finish a task faster is a satisfying experience for me.

nodes.label: Accuracy and factual correctness
nodes.grounding_span: I always collaborate with AI to ensure that everything it provides is correct and factual.

nodes.label: Data privacy and security
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to do any tasks that include confidential information as I worry about how my data is shared.

nodes.label: Personal skill maintenance and growth
nodes.grounding_span: I do worry that my writing will get worse if I rely too much on AI to complete my writing tasks for me.

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for time-saving
nodes.grounding_span: Any time I can use AI to finish a task faster is a satisfying experience for me.

nodes.label: Frustration with AI errors in calculations
nodes.grounding_span: I once used AI to calculate budget figures and it repeatedly made mistakes. The most frustrating part was that I knew the numbers it was providing were wrong but I couldn't figure out where it was making mistakes.

nodes.label: Caution due to colleagues' blind trust
nodes.grounding_span: I have seen my colleagues blindly trust AI without checking responses, which has led to issues with incorrect information being provided. This has made me be extra diligent in making sure I always fact-check and read over all AI responses if using them in my work.

nodes.label: Concern about skill degradation
nodes.grounding_span: I do worry that my writing will get worse if I rely too much on AI to complete my writing tasks for me.

nodes.label: Skepticism about future delegation
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think AI will get to a point in the distant future where I will feel comfortable delegating entire tasks to it rather than collaborating and fact-checking its responses.

nodes.label: Iterative collaborative problem-solving
nodes.grounding_span: I would first state what I'm trying to accomplish and ask what formula I need. The AI would provide a list of formulas and explain why each one could work; I would pick which formula I thought would work the best and try it in my spreadsheet. Depending on whether or not it was successful, I would continue the conversation with AI and explain what was happening and ask for further help.

nodes.label: Vigilant oversight and fact-checking
nodes.grounding_span: I always collaborate with AI to ensure that everything it provides is correct and factual.
